data.title: US FDA puts partial clinical hold on PepGen's muscle disease drug trial
data.description:
PepGen said on Wednesday that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration has placed a partial clinical hold on its mid‑stage trial of a rare muscle disease drug, sending its shares plunging over 25% in extended trading.

The drug, PGN‑EDODM1, is being tested for myotonic dystrophy type 1, a genetic disease that causes muscles to weaken and tighten over time, making movement and daily activities harder.

PepGen said the health regulator's concerns relate to earlier laboratory and animal studies the company submitted.

The agency did not flag any safety concerns from patient data generated in an early-stage study.

PepGen said it is working with the FDA to address the questions and will submit additional information, including newly unblinded data from the earlier study.

While the U.S. portion of the trial is on hold, the company has been cleared to open the study in South Korea, Australia and New Zealand.

Patients in the UK and Canada are already being treated at the 10 mg/kg dose, following a recommendation from an independent safety board to move ahead with dose escalation.

No patients in the U.S. had been enrolled in the trial before the hold was placed.

PepGen said it had $148.5 million in cash and investments as of December 31, 2025, enough to fund its operations into the second half of 2027.

data.title: CompX International Q4 revenue dips due to lower Security Products sales
data.description:
Overview

  • Security products and marine components maker's Q4 net sales fell yr/yr due to lower healthcare sales

  • Q4 operating income rose yr/yr, driven by higher Marine Components sales and improved margins

Outlook

  • CompX International did not provide specific guidance for future periods in press release

Result Drivers

  • SECURITY PRODUCTS DECLINE - Q4 net sales decreased due to lower Security Products sales to the healthcare market

  • MARINE COMPONENTS GROWTH - Higher Marine Components sales to the industrial market boosted Q4 operating income

  • FULL-YEAR SALES INCREASE - Full-year sales growth driven by higher Security Products sales to government market and Marine Components sales to multiple markets

data.title: NN Inc. Q4 net sales slightly miss expectations
data.description:
Overview

  • Industrial components maker's Q4 net sales slightly missed analyst expectations

  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q4 missed analyst expectations

Outlook

  • NN expects 2026 revenue between $445 mln and $465 mln

  • Company forecasts 2026 adjusted EBITDA between $50 mln and $60 mln

  • NN anticipates new business wins of $70 mln to $80 mln in 2026

Result Drivers

  • BUSINESS RATIONALIZATION - Co attributed improved operating and net loss to rationalization of underperforming business and plants, and lower compensation expenses

  • NEW BUSINESS WINS - Co secured approximately $70 mln in new business wins in 2025, exceeding guidance and contributing to future growth

  • TRANSFORMATION COMPLETION - Co completed capital-intensive transformation plan, including plant closures and headcount realignment, positioning for future growth
